title: Senate Bill 227, a measure by Senator Brian W. Jones (R-Santee) to save off-highway vehicle (OHV) competitions in California, was approved by a key Senate Committee.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Red Sticker program under the California Air Resources Board has allowed competition off-highway vehicles to operate in California for the past two decades.",
    "The Red Sticker program is set to end this year with no plan to replace it.",
    "Without a new program, off-highway vehicle competitions and practice riding on public lands will end in California.",
    "The loss of off-highway vehicle competitions will result in a great loss to local businesses and competitors.",
    "Funding for environmental work and law enforcement related to off-highway vehicle operations will also be lost.",
    "SB 227 creates a new off-highway vehicle identification program under the California Department of Parks and Recreation, tailored for legitimate competitors only.",
    "OHV competitions are a traditional sport that is important to the economies of suburban and rural California."
  ]
}
```
